what_it_does: A prescriptive, draft-for-approval master proposal for fusing OpenWebUI and the Company substrate. It synthesizes area maps and capability-level fusion drafts into one spine meant for Tim's approval and subsequent build execution.
observations: Declares a 'no source of truth / nothing complete / no horizon' principle and an inward-facing 'no duplicates' rule.
Lists five verified-live broken foundations (F1–F5) that block any fusion work.
Proposes seven capability-level fusions: channels, brain+tools, voice+ears, conversations+sessions, knowledge+memory, identity+users, faces+surfaces.
Includes a company-internal coherence ledger of ten duplication/half-build issues (I1–I10) to be healed in the same pass.
Provides an eight-step build sequence starting with foundations and claiming each step is reversible.
Contains explicit corrections to prior assumptions (voice shim is correct, company has no conversation store, identity is thinner than assumed).
Ends with an 'honest status' section distinguishing verified-live, assumed/map-sourced, and not-trusted claims.
standouts: The 'no source of truth / no horizon' stance plus the inward 'no duplicates' rule that treats company self-duplication as part of the fusion work.
The five verified broken foundations are framed as current breakage, not design choices.
The proposal to build a new company conversation store using OpenWebUI's branching DAG shape as a view, because the company currently has none.
The identity fusion: one principal registry with AGENT and VIEWER kinds, gated by OpenWebUI's AccessGrant as the single authority.
conventions: YAML front matter with type, title, register, status, posture, date, and grounds.
Numbered sections with capability summaries and a tabular internal-issues ledger.
Inline 'Correction:' callouts that revise earlier assumptions.
An explicit epistemic-status section separating verified, assumed, and not-trusted claims.
concerns: Draft status means the whole plan is provisional pending Tim's approval.
Several seams are flagged for live verification before build (e.g., OpenWebUI MCP client HTTP gateway fit).
The build sequence claims reversibility but interdependencies may make true rollback nontrivial.
No concrete code symbols, schemas, or interfaces are defined in this file; it remains a high-level spine.
Some claims rely on OpenWebUI internals that were not re-verified live this pass.
notes: A high-level but unusually self-aware fusion spine that fixes broken foundations first, heals internal duplication as it goes, and refuses to declare a final destination.
questions: What are the exact schemas and contents of the per-capability fusion files listed in the grounds?
What specific OpenWebUI API/build details does 'RHM-as-Pipe' require beyond the manifold concept?
How is OpenWebUI's AccessGrant model enforced and mapped to company posture tiers?
What criteria will be used to choose among the five face/surface options?
Which exact OWUI version and frontend bundle constraints apply to each face option?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is an integrated, approval-oriented fusion proposal and then delivers verified blockers, capability syntheses, internal heals, and a sequenced build plan.
apparent_themes: No single source of truth; synthesis from incomplete systems.
Inward healing of duplication as part of outward integration.
Live verification over trust of code self-description.
OpenWebUI as both donor of parts and face onto a company centre that accrues.
Epistemic honesty about what is verified, assumed, or not trusted.
intention_signals: To secure Tim's approval for a unified, sequenced build plan that merges OpenWebUI's product-grade surfaces into the Company substrate while cleaning up the company's own duplication and live breakage.
novelty: Its explicit 'no source of truth / no horizon' stance and the inward-facing 'no duplicates' rule that treats company self-duplication as part of the same fusion work, plus an honest status ledger separating verified from assumed claims.
contribution: A structured, approval-ready fusion spine that names verified blockers, proposes capability-level syntheses, catalogs internal duplication to heal, and sequences reversible build steps.
summary_for_embedding: A master fusion proposal document prescribing how to integrate OpenWebUI with the Company by combining best parts of both into the company substrate, fixing live broken foundations, healing internal duplications, and sequencing reversible build steps for Tim's approval.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a living, approval-oriented build spine for fusing OpenWebUI and Company capabilities while maintaining epistemic honesty and a no-duplicates discipline.
